LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EMILIE CALHOUN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH 15 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMPLAINT, AS PLED, IS UNCERTAIN, AMBIGUOUS, AND UNINTELLIGIBLE. (CCP SECTION 430.10(F).) THEREFORE, THE COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER, WITH 15 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, TO PROVIDE PLAINTIFFS WITH AN OPPORTUNITY TO STATE SPECIFICALLY THE CAUSES OF ACTIONS ALLEGED AGAINST DEFENDANT AND THE FACTS WHICH SUPPORT THE ALLEGED CAUSES OF ACTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489; DEPT. 302. =302/EHG

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS PATRICK MCMANUS AND KATHERINE MCMANUS' PETITION TO VACATE SAN FRANCISCO BAR FEE ARBITRATION IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 5, 2016 AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE THE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. THE MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JANUARY 22, 2016 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. NOT REPORTED. (302/EG)

MOTION TO TAX COSTS
Off Calendar May-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kyle Montes de Oca of Roeca Haas Hager LLP telephone number 415-352-0980 for Defendant Emilie Calhoun.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF PATRICK MCMANUS' MOTION TO TAX COSTS is denied in its entirety. Defendant Emilie Calhoun is entitled to costs per CCP 1032(a)(4). All of the costs in the costs memorandum were reasonably necessary to the defense of this case, are reasonable in amount, and are not among the costs that are made unrecoverable by CCP 1033.5(b) and thus are allowable per CCP 1033.5(a)(1) and (c). Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change To Unlimited Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Feb-24-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff PATRICK MCMANUS, Tel No. 510-318-2982 appearing in pro per;
No other appearances made in this matter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFFS PATRICK MCMANUS AND KATHERINE MCMANUS' Motion For Change To Unlimited Jurisdiction, Plaintiffs' motion for reclassification of this case as an unlimited case is granted. None of the procedural errors including the lack of timely filing of this motion assertedly committed by plaintiffs discussed in defendant's opposition memorandum caused any prejudice to defendant and thus are not grounds to deny the motion. Plaintiffs seek damages of more than $25,000 in their second amended complaint, which makes this case presumptively an unlimited case. Defendant's argument that most of plaintiffs' alleged damages lack merit is not grounds to deny the motion. Denial of a motion to reclassify to an unlimited case is rarely warranted and must not be based on a determination of the merits of the case to avoid the possible impairment of plaintiffs' right to recovery. (Weil & Brown, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2015) 3:119 et. seq. and cases cited therein). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-16-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiffs PATRICK MCMANUS and KATHERINE MCMANUS, Tel No. 510-318-2982 appearing in pro per (LIANA MCMANUS also present);
Defendant EMILIE CALHOUN, ESQ., Tel No. 415-759-8755 appearing in pro per.

Matter argued and submitted by parties. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT EMILIE CALHOUN'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled in its entirety. Ms. Calhoun's request for judicial notice is granted. Plaintiffs' legal malpractice claims against Ms. Calhoun are not co-extensive with the issues that were adjudicated in the parties' attorney's fees arbitration nor, due to Business and Professions Code 6203(a), could plaintiffs have raised all of their grounds supporting Ms. Calhoun's asserted malpractice in the arbitration proceeding. Therefore, neither plaintiffs' failure to allege grounds to vacate the arbitration award or the doctrine of res judicata bar plaintiffs' claims as a matter of law. Because the face of the second amended complaint does not disclose that plaintiffs sustained actual injury as a result of Ms. Calhoun's asserted malpractice more than a year before this lawsuit was filed, Ms. Calhoun's statute of limitations argument lacks merit. Plaintiff's allegation that they "missed the statute of limitations to reverse the probate" due to Ms. Calhoun's asserted malpractice is not definitively disproved by the Alameda Court records and thus must be accepted as true on this demurrer. Plaintiffs shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature to be e-mailed to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Hearing Of Petition To Vacate San Francisco Bar Fee Arbitration
Off Calendar Feb-05-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiffs PATRICK MCMANUS and KATHERINE MCMANUS, Tel No. 510-318-2982 appearing in pro per (non-party LIANA MCMANUS also present);
Defendant EMILE M. CALHOUN, ESQ., Tel No. 415-759-8755 appearing in pro per.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS PATRICK MCMANUS AND KATHERINE MCMANUS' Petition To Vacate San Francisco Bar Fee Arbitration is denied. Plaintiffs' petition to vacate he petition is untimely. Plaintiffs failed to serve the petition on defendant within 100 days after service of the award as required by Code of Civil Procedure 1288. Moreover, plaintiffs fail to establish any of the one of the limited grounds for vacating an awarding set forth in CCP 1286.2. Court grants Plaintiffs' request for a statement of decision. Court shall issue a statement of decision. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-06-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - PATRICK MCMANUS & KATHERINE MCMANUS, TEL. NO. 1-510-318-2982, PLAINTIFFS APPEARING IN PRO PER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: DEFENDANT EMILIE CALHOUN's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Demurrer sustained, with 15 days leave to amend. The complaint, as pled, is uncertain, ambiguous, and unintelligible. (CCP section 430.10(f).) Therefore, the court sustains the demurrer, with 15 days leave to amend, to provide Plaintiffs with an opportunity to state specifically the causes of actions alleged against Defendant and the facts which support the alleged causes of action. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
